etymology.description: semantic 艸(艸) (cǎo, 'grass') + phonetic 兹(兹) (zī) -> indicates something present or currently happening
etymology.interpretation: The character 茲(zī, 'now') combines the grass 艸(艸) (cǎo, 'grass') to suggest growth or presence with the phonetic 兹(兹) (zī) which provides the sound. This pairing thus is used to denote something present or current, metaphorically like growing grass.

examples.chinese: 他茲辞去了职务(他茲辭去了職務)。
examples.english: He now resigned from his position.

examples.chinese: 公司决定茲发布新的政策(公司決定茲發布新的政策)。
examples.pinyin: Gōngsī juédìng zī fābù xīn de zhèngcè.
examples.english: The company has decided to now announce a new policy.

examples.chinese: 情况茲变得复杂(情況茲變得複雜)。
examples.english: The situation has now become complicated.
